View all files | | | | ## Repository files navigation * README More items # CV Generator A lightweight Python toolkit for generating clean, ATS-friendly PDF CVs tailored to specific job descriptions — one script per application. Python License --- ## Why Most CV tools force you into rigid templates or online editors. This toolkit gives you **full control in plain Python**: * One `profile.yaml` for personal details — fill in once * One `base_resume.py` for your complete work history — write all your bullets once * One script per application — cherry-pick and bold what fits each JD * PDF output via ReportLab — no browser, no account, no cloud --- ## Quick Start # 1. Clone git clone https://github.com/your-username/cv-generator.git cd cv-generator # 2. Install dependencies pip install -r requirements.txt # 3. Fill in your personal details nano profile.yaml # name, email, phone, LinkedIn, location # 4. Add your full work history nano base_resume.py # all your jobs, bullets, education, awards # 5. Copy the example and tailor it to a specific JD cp example_backend_engineer.py generate_cv_mycompany.py nano generate_cv_mycompany.py # 6. Generate python3 generate_cv_mycompany.py # → outputs/CV_Jane_Smith_Acme_Senior_Backend_Engineer.pdf --- ## Project Structure ``` cv-generator/ ├── CLAUDE.md # instructions for Claude Code (the AI workflow) ├── profile.yaml # personal info: name, email, phone, LinkedIn (git-ignored) ├── base_cv_template.md # your full resume in markdown — source of truth (git-ignored) │ # OR drop base_cv.pdf here instead (git-ignored) ├── base_resume.py # structured resume data imported by CV scripts (git-ignored) ├── cv_builder.py # PDF rendering engine (no need to edit) ├── update_sheet.py # Google Sheets application logger ├── sheet_config.yaml.example # copy → sheet_config.yaml and fill in your Sheet ID ├── example_backend_engineer.py # fully worked example showing the workflow ├── requirements.txt ├── outputs/ # generated PDFs land here (git-ignored) └── README.md ``` --- ## How It Works — Three Layers ### Layer 1 — `profile.yaml` · Personal details Fill this in once. Every script reads your name, contact info, and accent colour from here automatically. name: "Jane Smith" email: "jane@example.com" phone: "+49 123 4567890" location: "Berlin, Germany" linkedin: "https://linkedin.com/in/janesmith" accent_color: "1a5276" # hex colour for headings and bullet points --- ### Layer 2 — `base_resume.py` · Your complete history Think of this as your **master resume** — every job you've held, every bullet you've ever written, all your education and awards. Nothing here is printed automatically; you import from it selectively in each script. # base_resume.py SKILLS = { "languages": "Go, Python, C++, Scala", "cloud": "AWS (EKS, S3, IAM), Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, Terraform", "messaging": "Kafka, Temporal — event-driven architecture at scale", "databases": "PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, Redis", "observability":"ELK stack, SLO/SLI/error budgets, distributed tracing", } JOBS = { "acme_senior": { "header": "Acme Corp — Senior Backend Engineer", "period": "Jan 2022 – Present | Berlin, Germany", "desc": "B2B SaaS platform — 500+ enterprise customers.", "bullets": [ # Write every bullet you could ever use for this role. # You'll pick the best ones per application. "Designed Kafka ingestion pipeline processing 2M events/day…", "Migrated monolith to Kubernetes (EKS) — cut release cycle to 2 days.", "Defined SLOs/SLIs and error budgets — 99.9% uptime.", "Led PostgreSQL zero-downtime migrations on 200M-row table…", ], }, # add more jobs… } EDUCATION = [ ("MSc Computer Science", "TU Berlin (2016–2018)", "Thesis: Distributed Consensus…"), ("BSc Computer Engineering", "University of Example (2012–2016)", ""), ] AWARDS = ["1st place — Hackathon 2023", "Top 10% — Advent of Code 2022"] --- ### Layer 3 — Per-role script · Tailored application Copy `example_backend_engineer.py`, rename it, and select/reorder content from `base_resume.py` to match the specific JD. Bold the keywords the JD uses. Adjust the summary and title. # generate_cv_mycompany.py from cv_builder import CVBuilder import base_resume as base cv = CVBuilder("outputs/CV_Jane_Smith_Acme_Engineer.pdf") # Title tailored to this JD cv.header("Senior Backend Engineer — Go, Kubernetes & Distributed Systems") # Summary rewritten for this role cv.summary( "Senior engineer with 8+ years building distributed systems " "in Go. Deep experience with Kubernetes and Kafka at " "10M+ user scale. Berlin-based, 1-month notice." ) # Skills — reorder to match the JD's preferred stack cv.skills([ ("Languages", "Go (primary), Python, C++"), ("Cloud", "AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform"), ("Databases", base.SKILLS["databases"]), # reuse from base ]) # Jobs — import from base, pick only the most relevant bullets acme = base.JOBS["acme_senior"] cv.job( acme["header"], acme["period"], acme["desc"], [ acme["bullets"][0], # Kafka — JD asks for event-driven acme["bullets"][1], # Kubernetes — JD asks for K8s acme["bullets"][2], # SLO/uptime — JD asks for SRE ] ) cv.education(base.EDUCATION) cv.awards(base.AWARDS) cv.build() python3 generate_cv_mycompany.py # → outputs/CV_Jane_Smith_MyCompany_Senior_Backend_Engineer.pdf --- ## Tailoring Tips ### Score the JD before writing Compare the JD requirements to your experience and assign a score before writing a single word. | Score | Signal | | ------- | ------------------------------------- | | 85–100% | Strong fit — lead with JD keywords | | 70–84% | Good fit — address gaps honestly | | 55–69% | Stretch — apply, flag gaps upfront | | < 55% | Skip or apply only if very interested | ### Language order Always list languages in the order the JD emphasises them — the recruiter's eye lands on the first word: # Backend/infra role (Go preferred) ("Languages", "Go, Python, C++") # ML/data role (Python preferred) ("Languages", "Python, Go, C++") ### Bold JD keywords inline Wrap exact JD terms in `…`. Both ATS parsers and recruiters scan for these: "Built event-driven architecture using Kafka and Temporal…" ### Ground every bullet in a number Weak: `"Improved system performance."` Strong: `"Reduced p99 latency from 800 ms to 60 ms serving 10M MAU."` --- ## Output PDFs are written to `outputs/`. The folder is git-ignored — your generated CVs stay local. --- ## Google Sheets Logging (optional) Track every application in a spreadsheet automatically. ### Setup 1. Create a Google Sheet with these headers in row 1: ``` Company | Applied By | Date | Status | Role | Link | Notes | Match ``` 2. Create a Google Cloud service account, enable the Sheets API, and download the JSON key as `service_account.json`. 3. Share your spreadsheet with the service account email (Editor access). 4. Copy the config example and fill in your Sheet ID: cp sheet_config.yaml.example sheet_config.yaml nano sheet_config.yaml 5. Install the extra dependencies: pip install google-auth google-api-python-client ### Usage python3 update_sheet.py \ --company "Acme Corp" \ --role "Senior Backend Engineer" \ --match 82 \ --link "https://jobs.acme.com/..." \ --status "Applied" # → Added: Acme Corp — Senior Backend Engineer (5/6/2026) | Match: 82% Both `service_account.json` and `sheet_config.yaml` are git-ignored — they never leave your machine. --- ## Using with Claude Code This project is designed to work with Claude Code. The `CLAUDE.md` file instructs Claude to run the full application workflow automatically. Just open this folder in Claude Code and say: ``` apply for https://jobs.acme.com/senior-backend-engineer ``` Claude will: 1. Fetch and parse the job description 2. Score the match against your base resume 3. Generate a tailored PDF CV 4. Log the row to your Google Sheet 5. Optionally auto-fill the application form (just say "auto-apply") See `CLAUDE.md` for the full workflow details. --- ## Requirements * Python 3.10+ * `reportlab >= 4.0.0` * `PyYAML >= 6.0` * _(optional)_ `google-auth >= 2.0.0` and `google-api-python-client >= 2.0.0` for sheet logging --- ## License MIT — use freely, attribution appreciated but not required. ## About No description, website, or topics provided. ### Resources Readme ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. Please reload this page. Activity ### Stars **4** stars ### Watchers **0** watching ### Forks **0** forks Report repository ## Releases No releases published ## Packages0 ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. Please reload this page. ## Contributors ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. Please reload this page. ## Languages * Python 100.0% ## Footer © 2026 GitHub, Inc. ### Footer navigation * Terms * Privacy * Security * Status * Community * Docs * Contact * Manage cookies * Do not share my personal information You can’t perform that action at this time. ____ Sunday, Jul 19, 2026, 7:54 PM (UTC+4) https://github.com/niazimojtaba/apply_agent see this repo and think that we can add something to our skills based on the experience here applied.
I'll compare that apply_agent workflow with our existing resume/application skills and propose what worth adopting.

[apply_agent](https://github.com/niazimojtaba/apply_agent) is a lean “apply for URL” loop: fetch JD → **score match** → cherry-pick bullets into a ReportLab script → PDF → optional Sheets log / form fill. Our `mcp-resume-reframe` skill is already stronger on infrastructure (catalog MCP, gated phases, additive master, project evidence). What’s worth stealing is mostly **decision discipline**, not the architecture.

## Already covered (don’t copy)

| Their idea | Ours |
|---|---|
| Never invent facts | Same golden rule + project masters |
| Skills reorder to JD | Phase 3 |
| Application logging | Panel / `mark_applied`, not Sheets |
| Pause before submit | N/A (no auto-apply tooling) |
| One tailored artifact per job | `save_tailored_tex` overwrite |

## Do **not** adopt

- **Cherry-pick / drop bullets** — opposite of our additive-only contract
- **Auto-run full pipeline** — we gate for a reason
- **Script-per-app + ReportLab** — we already have LaTeX + panel PDF
- **Browser auto-apply** — different product; only if we build it later

## Worth adding to the skill

**1. Requirements × match matrix + numeric score (highest value)**  
Phase 1 today: company thesis, top keywords, ATS yes/no. Their CLAUDE.md forces a table *before any CV writing*:

```text
## Match Score: 82 / 100
| Requirement | Match |
| Go (primary) | ✅ Primary |
| React / TS   | ❌ Gap |
```

Thresholds: 85+ strong / 70–84 good / 55–69 stretch / **<55 flag before continuing**. Fits our gated flow as part of phase 1 (or a short phase 1b).

**2. Lead-bullet rule**  
They: first bullet = strongest JD match. We already put new mirror bullets on top — make it explicit: the **first** new (or existing lead) bullet must carry the primary JD keyword cluster.

**3. JD-keyword emphasis in LaTeX**  
They bold with `<b>`. We should say: wrap exact JD terms in `\textbf{…}` sparingly in **new** mirror bullets + summary — not every word on the master.

**4. Role-type skill-order cheat sheet**  
Concrete table (backend → Go first, ML → Python first, etc.) to tighten phase 3 reorder — we currently say “reorder” without examples.

**5. Post-save decision summary**  
After acceptance: 3 bullets — what we mirrored, what we bolded/reordered, what stays interview-only. Slight upgrade to the current closing line.

## Skip for now

- Form auto-fill / `apply.py` — only if you want a separate skill + Playwright tooling  
- Google Sheets — panel already owns tracking  
- Fetch-URL-in-chat — we correctly use `get_job_context` / paste path

---

**Recommendation:** extend `mcp-resume-reframe` with items **1–5**, mainly the match matrix + thresholds in phase 1. Small skill edit, no MCP/schema changes.

Want me to draft that into the skill (+ `pipeline-prompts.md` if needed)? Or only the match-score piece first?

content.text:
**Considering ATS integration**

It’s intriguing that there’s an ATS score existing. I think I should explore integrating the `set_ats_score`. The previous recommendation didn't highlight schema changes, but the skill could potentially call the `set_ats_score`. I might need to inspect the skill's rest to see if `set_ats_score` is currently included. This could help ensure everything aligns properly, so I’ll take a closer look at that.

**Considering tool integration**

I think we should integrate the set_ats_score function in phase 1 since the user suggested adding a proposed score from 1 to 5. I could add numeric values and likely save the score to the existing tool. But first, I need to inspect the set_ats_score tool semantics and check out the relevant documentation for guidance on how to implement this properly. It’s important I do it right!

**Evaluating technical processes**

It looks like phase 1 currently only recognizes masters without loading related content. The existing skill bootstrap should retrieve the best master slug before progressing. In the pipeline, slot 1 specifies listing names only and claiming the current master guess, making scoring against content impossible. I need to adjust slot 1 to retrieve the best slug for evidence matching while still allowing phase 2 to load candidates. There's also a need for important adjustments and clarifications regarding the role-type skill order cheat sheet. Let’s get to work on those patches!

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/SKILL.md
@@
 ### Phase 1 — JD + ATS lens
 
 Use **`get_job_context(...).description_text`** as the JD (not the posting URL). Act as a tired hiring manager + ATS scanner for **this role only**.
 
 - **Company thesis:** what product/problem space are they in? (2 sentences)
 - **JD must-haves:** top 8 keywords/skills the screen will hunt for
+- **Requirements × evidence matrix:** load the most likely master with `get_master_resume`, then score the top requirements against explicit master/project evidence. Show requirement, weight, evidence, verdict (`strong` / `partial` / `gap`), and points earned. Weights must total 100; explicit must-haves outweigh nice-to-haves. Do not award points for inferred or unsupported experience.
+- **Match score:** total the matrix as `N / 100` and classify it:
+  - `85–100` — strong fit
+  - `70–84` — good fit
+  - `55–69` — stretch; state the decisive gaps
+  - `<55` — weak fit; flag it clearly before asking whether to continue
 - **Implicit signals:** 3 things not spelled out in the JD but likely scored
 - **ATS verdict:** would the **unchanged** master pass? Yes/no + one sentence why
 - **Mirror target:** which domain story should dominate the CV? (e.g. "payments", "monolith→microservices migration", "search at scale")
 
 **Stop. Ask to go ahead.**
@@
 - Optional **summary/profile** tweak (2–3 sentences) — show before/after; master text stays if user declines
-- **Mirror role:** propose **1–2 new bullets** (Google XYZ) for JD similarity — placed **at the top** of that role's bullet list. Prefer facts grounded in the chosen **project master** when relevant. **List every existing master bullet** for that role unchanged below them
+- **Mirror role:** propose **1–2 new bullets** (Google XYZ) for JD similarity — placed **at the top** of that role's bullet list. The first new bullet must carry the strongest truthful JD keyword cluster. Prefer facts grounded in the chosen **project master** when relevant. **List every existing master bullet** for that role unchanged below them
+- **Keyword emphasis:** in proposed new bullets and approved summary text only, mark a small number of exact JD terms for later `\textbf{...}` emphasis. Never alter existing master bullets merely to add emphasis, and never bold unsupported terms.
 - Optional **short Projects LaTeX block** only if the user wants it and/or the master template already has Projects — prefer copying/condensing the project master's LaTeX; never dump an unedited giant block without approval
 - **Skills reorder proposal:** reorder only — same skill lines as master, JD-relevant lines moved up
 - Flag anything that feels like a stretch — user decides whether to keep it
 
+Default skill-order cues (the JD's explicit order always wins):
+
+| Role type | Lead with |
+|-----------|-----------|
+| Backend / platform / infrastructure | Primary backend language, distributed systems/cloud, data stores |
+| ML / AI / data engineering | Python/data languages, ML/data stack, cloud/data stores |
+| Systems / embedded | C/C++ or named systems language, low-level/runtime skills, tooling |
+| Engineering management | Team's dominant technical domain, leadership/delivery, architecture |
+
 **Stop. Ask to go ahead.**
@@
-1. Convert to full tailored LaTeX — **same document class, packages, macros, and section order** as the chosen master
+1. Convert to full tailored LaTeX — **same document class, packages, macros, and section order** as the chosen master. Apply approved JD emphasis with `\textbf{...}` only in new mirror bullets and approved summary text.
@@
-Close with: master slug used, mirror role, 1-line gap reminder for the interview, save status.
+Close with:
+
+- **Mirrored:** master slug, mirror role, and bullets added
+- **Emphasized:** approved keyword emphasis and skill-line reordering
+- **Interview-only:** one-line truthful gap reminder
+- **Saved:** save/validation status, including whether an earlier tailored file was overwritten
*** End Patch

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/pipeline-prompts.md
@@
-Phase 1 only. Sources: get_job_context (use description_text as the JD — do not open the posting URL) + list_master_resumes (names only). If has_description is false, stop and ask me to fetch the JD on the panel first.
+Phase 1 only. Sources: get_job_context (use description_text as the JD — do not open the posting URL) + list_master_resumes + get_master_resume for the most likely master. If has_description is false, stop and ask me to fetch the JD on the panel first.
 
 Act as ATS + hiring manager for THIS role.
 - Company thesis (2 sentences): what they build and what this hire owns.
 - JD must-haves: top 8 keywords/skills a screener will match.
+- Requirements × evidence matrix: requirement, weight, explicit master/project evidence, verdict (strong/partial/gap), and points. Weights total 100; must-haves outweigh nice-to-haves; unsupported experience earns no points.
+- Match score: N/100. Classify 85–100 strong, 70–84 good, 55–69 stretch, <55 weak. For stretch/weak, state the decisive gaps clearly before asking to continue.
 - Implicit signals: 3 likely scored themes not explicit in the JD.
-- ATS verdict: would my current master (best guess slug) pass unchanged? yes/no + why.
+- ATS verdict: would the loaded master pass unchanged? yes/no + why.
 - Mirror target: one domain story the CV should lead with (e.g. retail-media migration, payments, search).
@@
 ADDITIVE ONLY — do not remove or shorten any master content.
 - Optional summary/profile tweak — show before/after; keep master text if I decline.
-- Mirror role: propose 1–2 NEW XYZ bullets for JD similarity (prepend at top of that role).
+- Mirror role: propose 1–2 NEW XYZ bullets for JD similarity (prepend at top of that role). The first new bullet carries the strongest truthful JD keyword cluster.
+- Keyword emphasis: mark a few exact JD terms in NEW bullets and approved summary text for later \textbf{...}; never alter existing master bullets just to add emphasis.
 - List EVERY existing bullet for that role from the master — unchanged, verbatim.
-- Skills: reorder proposal only — same lines as master, JD-relevant moved up.
+- Skills: reorder proposal only — same lines as master, JD-relevant moved up. Default cues: backend/platform → primary backend language, cloud/distributed systems, data stores; ML/data → Python/data stack, ML/data systems, cloud; systems/embedded → C/C++ or named systems language, low-level/runtime, tooling; management → team's technical domain, leadership/delivery, architecture. The JD's explicit order wins.
 - Flag any new bullet that feels like a stretch; I decide.
@@
 - Convert approved draft to full LaTeX using the chosen master's preamble, macros, and section order (not a new template).
 - COPY all master bullets verbatim; only prepend approved new mirror bullets.
+- Apply approved \textbf{...} emphasis only in new mirror bullets and approved summary text.
 - get_job_context(country, company, url) — use the url/country/company it returns for save.
@@
-Close with: slug, mirror role, bullets added, save status (note if overwritten).
+Close with:
+- Mirrored: slug, mirror role, bullets added.
+- Emphasized: approved JD terms and skill-line reordering.
+- Interview-only: one-line truthful gap reminder.
+- Saved: save/validation status (note if overwritten).
*** End Patch

content.text:
**Reviewing command outputs**

It seems like I need to check command outputs. I can use `git diff --check -- paths` and maybe `git diff --stat` to examine things closely. I wonder if it’s necessary to run tests for documentation, though. I’ll definitely want to make sure to check lints using a tool. Ah, parallel shell might help with lints too. It feels like I’m getting organized with these commands!

**Investigating git diff issue**

I'm noticing an empty git diff stat, and I wonder why that is. Could it be that there are ignored files? The .claude skill files might be untracked, and the git status snapshot didn’t show them. I should check the .gitignore for .claude. Maybe the ApplyPatch changed but isn't tracked by git? I need to verify this using git status --short and git ls-files since shell commands are allowed. Let's figure this out!

**Evaluating skill changes**

It seems like the user is interested in adding a skill, but I need to consider that the local environment has changed. I'm wondering if the skill could be linked with symlinks. Using `ls` might be okay for directory verification, but file operations feel disallowed. So, I think I’ll also look into the `git check-ignore -v` command to check for ignored files. That could help clarify the situation!

content.name: ApplyPatch

content.text:
Updated both:

- `.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/SKILL.md`
- `.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/pipeline-prompts.md`

Added:
- Weighted requirements/evidence matrix and match score
- Fit thresholds and gap handling
- Score recalculation when master selection changes
- Strongest-match lead bullet rule
- Selective JD keyword emphasis
- Role-specific skills ordering
- Structured post-save summary

No lint errors. Note: `.claude/` is gitignored, so these are local skill changes and won’t appear in commits.
